mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within one month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows, or the cross-sectional correlation between INDPRO and HOUST breaks down without affecting SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend while capacity utilization remains constrained, supply bottlenecks compress profit margins and force equity repricing downward, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO breaches the threshold, indicating that supply strength translates to earnings growth rather than margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over a 60-day horizon as fundamental valuation anchors shift higher.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns, or the signal's information coefficient drops below zero across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 250 while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real interest rates turn deeply negative, triggering a repricing of duration risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation and tight policy, or the joint signal produces a negative information coefficient in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms accelerating labor demand, which boosts near-term consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict 5-day SPX returns, or payroll growth decouples from unemployment declines without generating equity market gains.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential construction spending signals a collapse in housing demand and broader economic confidence, forcing equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5 percent over three months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite a housing spending contraction, or the signal shows no statistical edge in predicting equity downside.
